records.ety: [F. pilogue, L. epilogus, fr. Gr. <?/ conclusion, fr. <?/ to say in addition; 'epi` upon, besides + <?/ to say. See Legend.]

defs: A speech or short poem addressed to the spectators and recited by one of the actors, after the conclusion of the play.
quotes: A good play no epilogue, yet . . . good plays prove the better by the help of good epilogues.

defs: The closing part of a discourse, in which the principal matters are recapitulated; a conclusion.
